title: calamagrostis chamissonis
intro: Calamagrostis chamissonis, also known as coastal bentgrass, is a species of grass native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and fungal infections. The health benefits of Calamagrostis chamissonis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against fungal and bacterial infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
